paragraph: Veltrano Biosciences Corporation completed its initial public offering on the Arcadian National Exchange on March 14, 2026, raising gross proceeds of approximately $312 million through the sale of 18.35 million shares at an offering price of $17.00 per share. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Strathmore Capital Markets, with co-managers Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Dunmore Equity Partners. Demand for the offering exceeded supply by a factor of 11.4 times, prompting the underwriting syndicate to exercise the full 15 percent overallotment option within 48 hours of pricing. Veltrano shares opened at $24.60 on the first day of trading, representing a 44.7 percent premium to the offering price, and closed the session at $23.18. The company intends to deploy net proceeds of approximately $289 million to fund Phase III clinical trials of VLT-904, its lead oncology candidate targeting non-small-cell carcinomas of the biliary tract, with the remainder allocated to working capital and general corporate purposes. The 180-day lock-up period applicable to officers, directors, and pre-IPO stockholders expires on September 10, 2026. Veltrano was founded in 2019 and is headquartered in Meridian Shores, Caledonia.

Calderon Industrial REIT, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 22.0 million shares of common stock at $20.00 per share on April 7, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $440 million before deduction of underwriting discounts and commissions. The transaction was led by sole book-runner Harcastle Investment Bank, with passive bookrunners Fenwick Meridian Group and Briarwood Securities participating in syndication. Calderon intends to use the offering proceeds, together with borrowings under a newly established $175 million revolving credit facility, to acquire a seed portfolio of 31 light-industrial and last-mile logistics properties totaling 8.4 million square feet across the Central Velantian corridor. The overallotment option, covering an additional 3.3 million shares, was exercised in full on April 10, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $506 million. Shares of Calderon Industrial REIT began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under the ticker symbol CDRI, opening at $21.45 and reaching an intraday high of $22.90 before settling at $21.75 on the first day. The company anticipates an annualized dividend yield of approximately 5.2 percent based on the offering price. All insider shares are subject to a 365-day lock-up expiring April 7, 2027.

Nexovant Technologies Ltd. announced the pricing of a follow-on public offering of 9.5 million ordinary shares at $54.00 per share on February 19, 2026, for gross proceeds of approximately $513 million. The offering comprised 6.0 million primary shares issued by Nexovant and 3.5 million secondary shares sold by existing institutional shareholders, including Valcroft Growth Equity Fund IV and Pennridge Venture Capital Partners. Strathmore Capital Markets acted as sole book-running manager. Nexovant intends to use its approximately $316 million in net primary proceeds to accelerate the buildout of its proprietary edge-inference infrastructure across 14 new metropolitan markets and to fund bolt-on acquisitions in the industrial automation software sector. The secondary sellers received all proceeds attributable to their shares, net of underwriting discounts. The offering represented a 7.8 percent discount to the prior-day closing price of $58.60 on the Aldermoor Exchange. No lock-up waiver was granted in connection with the transaction. The company's remaining officers and directors, holding approximately 12.4 million shares in aggregate, remain subject to a 90-day lock-up restriction that expires on May 20, 2026. Nexovant reported trailing-twelve-month revenues of $1.14 billion as of December 31, 2025.

Torchfield Energy Partners LP priced an underwritten secondary offering of 14.2 million common units representing limited partner interests at $31.50 per unit on January 28, 2026, for aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $447.3 million. All units in the offering were sold by Torchfield's general partner affiliate, Meridian Basin Holdings LLC, and no proceeds were received by the partnership itself. Garvey Lynch & Co. served as sole book-running manager for the transaction. The units were sold at a 4.1 percent discount to the 10-day volume-weighted average price of $32.85. Following completion of the offering, Meridian Basin Holdings LLC's aggregate ownership interest in Torchfield Energy Partners LP declined from 38.4 percent to 22.9 percent on a fully diluted basis. Torchfield units are listed on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under the ticker symbol TFEP and closed at $32.10 on the day preceding pricing. The partnership operates approximately 4,800 miles of midstream gathering and processing infrastructure serving the Granville Shale basin and reported distributable cash flow of $618 million for the twelve months ended September 30, 2025, representing 1.41 times coverage of the annualized distribution rate of $2.80 per unit.

Peloric Consumer Brands, Inc. launched its initial public offering on May 2, 2026, pricing 25.0 million shares of Class A common stock at $15.00 per share for gross proceeds of $375 million, with an overallotment option covering 3.75 million additional shares granted to underwriters. Joint book-running managers Dunmore Equity Partners and Fenwick Meridian Group led the offering, with co-managers Albrecht Whitmore Securities and Ravensdale Capital rounding out the syndicate. Peloric, whose branded portfolio spans premium oral care, functional beverages, and direct-to-consumer wellness devices, intends to use net proceeds of approximately $344 million to repay in full outstanding indebtedness under its senior secured term loan, with the balance retained for strategic acquisitions and working capital. The company reported net revenues of $892 million for fiscal year 2025, representing 22 percent compound annual growth over the prior three-year period. The offering was 9.7 times oversubscribed, according to syndicate bookrunners, and shares opened at $19.40 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange on their first day of trading, closing at $18.65, a 24.3 percent premium to the IPO price. Officers, directors, and holders of 5 percent or more of outstanding shares are subject to a 180-day lock-up expiring October 29, 2026.

Stellara Renewable Power Corp. priced $500 million aggregate principal amount of 6.375 percent senior unsecured notes due 2033 in a private placement pursuant to Rule 144A and Regulation S on March 3, 2026. Harcastle Investment Bank and Strathmore Capital Markets served as joint lead arrangers and book-running managers for the debt offering. The notes were priced at par and will bear interest at a fixed rate of 6.375 percent per annum, payable semi-annually on March 3 and September 3 of each year, commencing September 3, 2026. Stellara intends to use the net proceeds of approximately $493 million, together with cash on hand, to fund the construction and interconnection of the Westmere Solar Complex, a 740-megawatt photovoltaic facility located in the Drysdale Basin, and to prepay $85 million of floating-rate borrowings under its existing senior secured revolving credit facility. The notes are expected to be rated BB+ by Meridian Credit Ratings and Ba1 by Thornfield Ratings Services. Covenants restrict Stellara from incurring additional indebtedness if the consolidated net leverage ratio exceeds 4.50 times trailing EBITDA. Stellara reported EBITDA of $314 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, implying pro forma leverage of approximately 3.8 times following the offering.

Quinterra Pharmaceuticals Inc. completed the pricing of a $275 million aggregate principal amount of 2.25 percent convertible senior notes due 2031 on April 22, 2026, in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers. Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Garvey Lynch & Co. acted as joint book-running managers. The notes were priced at par with an initial conversion premium of 30 percent above the reference price of $48.20 per Quinterra share, implying an initial conversion price of approximately $62.66 per share. Quinterra also granted the initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $41.25 million principal amount of notes, which was exercised in full, bringing total proceeds to approximately $316.25 million. Net proceeds of approximately $308 million will be used to fund continued clinical development of QNT-117, a bispecific antibody targeting autoimmune indications, and to enter into capped call transactions intended to reduce potential dilution at conversion. The capped call transactions have an initial cap price of approximately $96.40 per share, representing a 100 percent premium to the reference share price. Quinterra shares closed at $49.75 on the Aldermoor Exchange on the day of pricing, down $0.35 from the prior close.

Vorspar Semiconductor Group AG priced its initial public offering of 30.0 million depositary receipts, each representing one-half of one ordinary share, at $22.00 per depositary receipt on February 4, 2026, for gross proceeds of $660 million. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Briarwood Securities, Dunmore Equity Partners, and Ravensdale Capital. An overallotment option covering 4.5 million additional depositary receipts was exercised in full on February 9, 2026, increasing total gross proceeds to $759 million. Vorspar, headquartered in Hartenfeld, Noravia, designs advanced power-management integrated circuits for electric-vehicle drivetrains and industrial motor-control applications. The company reported revenues of $1.87 billion for fiscal year 2025, with adjusted EBITDA of $523 million, representing a 27.9 percent margin. Depositary receipts began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under the symbol VSRG on February 5, 2026, opening at $30.10 and closing at $28.55, a 29.8 percent first-day gain. Vorspar intends to use net proceeds of approximately $718 million to expand its Torvik wafer-fabrication facility by 60,000 square feet, fund research and development of its next-generation SiC power-stage architecture, and for general corporate purposes. Insiders are subject to a 180-day lock-up period.

Korinthia Hospitality Group, Inc. priced an upsized initial public offering of 16.0 million shares of common stock at $18.00 per share on March 25, 2026, raising gross proceeds of $288 million, an increase of $36 million from the previously announced offering size of $252 million. The company operates a portfolio of 74 select-service and extended-stay hotel properties totaling 9,610 rooms across the Solvent Coast and Inland Plateau regions. Sole book-runner Fenwick Meridian Group allocated shares primarily to long-only institutional investors, with hedge funds receiving less than 8 percent of the deal. An overallotment option covering 2.4 million shares was granted to underwriters and remains outstanding. Korinthia shares were admitted to trading on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker KRHS on March 26, 2026, and closed at $20.30 on the first day, representing a 12.8 percent premium to the offering price. Net proceeds of approximately $264 million will fund the acquisition of 12 additional properties under executed purchase agreements totaling $198 million in aggregate consideration, with the remainder applied to reduce outstanding borrowings under the company's $150 million revolving credit facility. Insiders holding approximately 21.3 million shares are subject to a 180-day lock-up restriction.

Aldira Capital Infrastructure Partners priced $800 million aggregate principal amount of 7.125 percent senior secured notes due 2034 on January 15, 2026, in a Rule 144A offering for institutional investors. The notes were issued at 99.50 percent of par, generating net proceeds of approximately $790 million after original issue discount and underwriting fees. Strathmore Capital Markets and Harcastle Investment Bank served as joint lead managers, with Albrecht Whitmore Securities as co-manager. The notes are secured on a first-lien basis by substantially all of the assets of Aldira Capital Infrastructure Partners and its material subsidiaries, including 17 operating toll-road concessions spanning 2,340 kilometers in the Valorian Republic and the Federal Territories of Morencourt. Proceeds will be used to refinance $650 million of senior secured notes bearing interest at 8.50 percent due 2028, fund a make-whole call premium estimated at $47 million, and provide approximately $93 million for general corporate purposes including maintenance capital expenditures. The transaction achieves annualized interest savings of approximately $11.0 million and extends the company's weighted average debt maturity from 2.3 years to 8.6 years. The notes are expected to carry a rating of BB from Thornfield Ratings Services and BB- from Meridian Credit Ratings.

Galveston Precision Agriculture Ltd. priced its initial public offering of 12.5 million shares at $24.00 per share on April 30, 2026, for gross proceeds of $300 million. The offering was 13.2 times oversubscribed, with orders received from more than 240 institutional investors across 19 countries. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Pelletier & Wynne Securities set the final offering price at the upper end of the $21.00 to $24.00 per share indicated price range. Galveston develops satellite-guided precision-application systems for row-crop farming and reported subscription-based software revenues of $148 million for fiscal year 2025, representing 41 percent year-over-year growth. The company intends to use net proceeds of approximately $276 million to fund expansion into 11 additional agricultural export markets, invest in fleet manufacturing capacity, and pursue tuck-in acquisitions of complementary data-analytics businesses. Shares opened at $33.50 on their first trading day on the Aldermoor Exchange under the symbol GVPA, reaching an intraday high of $35.10 before closing at $31.85, a 32.7 percent premium to the IPO price. Officers and directors are restricted from selling shares for 180 days, expiring October 27, 2026. Pre-IPO institutional shareholders holding 8.0 million shares in aggregate are subject to a 90-day restriction.

Thornbridge Specialty Insurance Holdings, Inc. completed its initial public offering on February 12, 2026, selling 20.0 million shares of common stock at $23.00 per share for gross proceeds of $460 million. Dunmore Equity Partners served as sole book-running manager. The deal included a full exercise of the 3.0 million share overallotment option by underwriters on February 15, 2026, resulting in total gross proceeds of $529 million. Thornbridge underwrites specialty property and casualty insurance covering marine cargo, political risk, and agricultural commodity exposures across 38 sovereign jurisdictions. The company reported gross written premiums of $1.23 billion for the year ended December 31, 2025, and a combined ratio of 89.4 percent, reflecting superior underwriting discipline relative to the specialty lines peer group. Net proceeds of approximately $487 million will be used to strengthen the company's risk-based capital position and fund the acquisition of Veradale Reinsurance Intermediaries Ltd. for a consideration of $320 million. Shares of Thornbridge began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker TBSH, closing at $25.90 on the first day of trading. Insiders and pre-IPO shareholders representing 72 percent of pre-offering shares outstanding are subject to a 180-day lock-up agreement.

Corellan Deepwater Corp. announced the completion of a $1.1 billion term loan B financing on March 19, 2026, consisting of a $900 million seven-year tranche and a $200 million five-year revolving credit facility. The transaction was arranged and syndicated by Harcastle Investment Bank as administrative agent, with Strathmore Capital Markets and Ravensdale Capital as joint lead arrangers. The term loan bears interest at a floating rate of SORF plus 325 basis points, with a 0.50 percent SORF floor, and was issued at an original issue discount of 99.00 cents on the dollar. Commitment fees on the undrawn revolving facility are set at 37.5 basis points per annum. Corellan intends to use term loan proceeds to fund the acquisition of Petrovantage Offshore Services Ltd. for an aggregate enterprise value of $820 million and to refinance approximately $235 million of existing senior secured credit facilities that were set to mature in August 2027. Pro forma net leverage following the transaction is estimated at 4.1 times trailing adjusted EBITDA of $268 million. The transaction closed substantially oversubscribed, with $2.4 billion in total commitments received from 58 participating lenders across the leveraged finance community. Corellan is rated B+ by Thornfield Ratings Services.

Lorriane Software Solutions, Inc. priced an upsized secondary offering of 8.0 million shares of Class B common stock at $91.00 per share on May 6, 2026, for aggregate gross proceeds of $728 million, all of which accrued to selling shareholders Vestmark Capital Fund II and Goldstream Technology Partners, with no proceeds flowing to the company. The offering size was increased from the originally announced 6.5 million shares based on investor demand. Garvey Lynch & Co. acted as sole book-running manager for the transaction, which priced at a 3.4 percent discount to the prior-day closing price of $94.20 on the Aldermoor Exchange. Lorriane reported annual recurring revenue of $540 million for the twelve months ended March 31, 2026, representing 36 percent growth year-over-year, and net revenue retention of 124 percent across its enterprise customer base. Following completion of the offering, Vestmark Capital Fund II's ownership stake declined from 18.7 percent to 9.1 percent, while Goldstream Technology Partners reduced its position from 11.4 percent to 4.2 percent. The company's remaining insiders, including co-founders Darian Westfall and Celeste Morvan, hold approximately 22 million shares and remain subject to a 90-day post-offering lock-up restriction expiring August 4, 2026.

Mariveld Green Hydrogen Corp. priced its initial public offering of 35.0 million shares at $11.00 per share on January 22, 2026, for gross proceeds of $385 million on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange. Joint book-running managers Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Albrecht Whitmore Securities led the transaction, which was 6.3 times oversubscribed. The overallotment option covering 5.25 million shares at the same price was exercised in full on January 27, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $442.75 million. Mariveld develops and operates large-scale alkaline electrolysis facilities producing industrial-grade green hydrogen from renewable electricity, with 380 megawatts of installed electrolyzer capacity currently operational across three sites. The company reported revenues of $162 million for fiscal year 2025, growing 58 percent from $103 million the prior year, and generated adjusted EBITDA of $44 million. Net proceeds will fund construction of two additional facilities in the Northgate Industrial Zone and Coastal Amber Region, each with 200 megawatts of planned capacity, at an aggregate capital cost of approximately $510 million. Shares opened at $14.20 on their first trading day and closed at $13.45, representing a 22.3 percent premium to the IPO price. Officers and directors are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions.

Castellan Financial Group Ltd. raised $650 million through the issuance of 6.875 percent subordinated notes due 2036 in a public offering registered under the Aldermoor Securities Act on April 14, 2026. Harcastle Investment Bank, Briarwood Securities, and Dunmore Equity Partners served as joint book-running managers for the transaction. The notes were priced at 100.00 percent of their $1,000 principal amount and will pay interest semi-annually on April 14 and October 14, commencing October 14, 2026. Castellan intends to use net proceeds of approximately $635 million to fund a liability management exercise consisting of a tender offer and consent solicitation for its outstanding 8.25 percent subordinated notes due 2029 at a consideration of $1,045 per $1,000 principal amount, with any remaining proceeds applied to general corporate purposes. The subordinated notes are not guaranteed by any subsidiary and are intended to qualify as Tier 2 regulatory capital under applicable provisions of the Aldermoor Prudential Framework for financial institutions. Castellan Financial Group reported total regulatory capital of $4.8 billion and a Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.7 percent as of December 31, 2025. The transaction was rated BB+ by Meridian Credit Ratings, consistent with the company's existing subordinated debt ratings.

Eastmark Logistics Technology, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 14.0 million shares of common stock at $28.00 per share on March 10, 2026, for gross proceeds of $392 million. Joint book-running managers Strathmore Capital Markets and Fenwick Meridian Group set the offering price at $28.00, above the $24.00 to $27.00 indicative price range established during the roadshow, reflecting exceptionally strong investor demand. The offering was 15.6 times oversubscribed. Eastmark operates a cloud-native freight-matching and transportation-management platform serving 4,800 shippers and 62,000 carrier partners across the Valorian Federal Economic Zone. The company reported revenues of $278 million for fiscal year 2025, up 47 percent year-over-year, with gross margins of 71 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $361 million will be allocated to sales and marketing expansion, research and development investment in autonomous dispatch technology, and strategic acquisitions. Shares of Eastmark began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker EMLT, opening at $41.50 and closing at $38.90, a 38.9 percent first-day gain. An overallotment option covering 2.1 million additional shares at $28.00 was exercised in full on March 13, 2026. All insiders holding shares prior to the offering are restricted from selling for 180 days, expiring September 6, 2026.

Vertshire Datacenters REIT priced a $400 million offering of 4.90 percent senior unsecured notes due 2032 on February 26, 2026, in a public transaction led by Garvey Lynch & Co. and Ravensdale Capital as joint book-running managers. The notes were priced at 99.75 percent of par to yield 4.936 percent to maturity, representing a spread of 148 basis points over the benchmark five-year Aldermoor Treasury rate of 3.456 percent. Vertshire intends to use net proceeds to fund the acquisition and development of two hyperscale data center campuses totaling 420,000 square feet of critical load capacity in the Noravia Free Trade Zone and the Solvent Coast Enterprise District. The company operated 1.84 million square feet of net rentable data center space as of December 31, 2025, with an occupancy rate of 97.3 percent and a weighted average remaining lease term of 8.4 years. Vertshire reported total revenues of $612 million and adjusted funds from operations of $2.87 per diluted share for fiscal year 2025. The notes are expected to be rated Baa2 by Thornfield Ratings Services and BBB by Meridian Credit Ratings, consistent with the company's investment-grade credit profile. Proceeds will also be used to pay fees and expenses associated with the transaction.

Dravenport Genomics, Inc. raised $180 million through a Rule 144A private placement of common stock at $36.00 per share, representing a 6.2 percent discount to the 30-day volume-weighted average price of $38.38, on April 3, 2026. The offering was structured as a PIPE transaction and did not require registration at the time of closing. Sole placement agent Pelletier & Wynne Securities ran an accelerated book-build process that was completed in under four hours, with demand from approximately 35 institutional investors including dedicated life sciences funds and crossover investors. Dravenport develops next-generation whole-genome sequencing instruments and reagent consumables for clinical diagnostic and population-health screening applications. The company reported total revenues of $127 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, with instrument placements of 318 units and consumable pull-through per installed instrument of $48,200 annually. Net proceeds of approximately $173 million will fund Phase II and Phase III pivotal studies of the company's GeneTrace liquid biopsy assay, expand manufacturing capacity at the Trentville production facility by 35 percent, and support international regulatory submissions in six new jurisdictions. Dravenport committed to file a resale registration statement covering the PIPE shares within 30 days of closing.

Selbourne Consumer Finance Corp. completed its initial public offering on May 14, 2026, pricing 18.5 million shares of common stock at $16.50 per share for gross proceeds of $305.25 million. Dunmore Equity Partners and Fenwick Meridian Group served as joint book-running managers, with Albrecht Whitmore Securities acting as co-manager. The overallotment option covering 2.775 million additional shares was partially exercised, with underwriters purchasing 2.1 million shares, bringing total gross proceeds to $339.9 million. Selbourne provides point-of-sale consumer financing and buy-now-pay-later installment products through an embedded finance platform integrated with approximately 12,400 merchant partners across the Valorian consumer economy. The company reported origination volumes of $3.2 billion for fiscal year 2025, reflecting 34 percent growth, net revenue of $244 million, and a net charge-off rate of 3.1 percent on managed receivables. Proceeds of approximately $294 million will be used to satisfy regulatory minimum capital requirements applicable to Selbourne's newly chartered consumer finance subsidiary, with approximately $45 million retained for general corporate purposes including technology investment. Shares began trading on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker SBCF, opening at $19.80 and closing at $19.10, a 15.8 percent first-day gain.

Morvan Specialty Chemicals PLC completed a £420 million sterling-denominated high-yield bond offering on March 31, 2026, issuing 7.500 percent senior notes due 2033 at an issue price of 99.000 percent of par. The offering was conducted pursuant to Rule 144A and Regulation S, with Harcastle Investment Bank and Strathmore Capital Markets acting as joint global coordinators and book-running managers. Morvan intends to use the sterling equivalent of approximately £398 million in net proceeds to finance the acquisition of Caldwell Adhesives & Sealants Ltd. for a total enterprise value of £585 million, and to refinance Caldwell's existing £180 million senior secured revolving credit facility. The combined entity will operate 14 manufacturing facilities across 9 countries and generate pro forma revenues of approximately £1.4 billion, with adjusted EBITDA of £210 million, implying a transaction multiple of 2.79 times enterprise value to EBITDA on a combined basis. Pro forma net leverage at closing is estimated at 4.2 times. The notes received a provisional rating of B+ from Thornfield Ratings Services. Morvan shares are listed on the Valorian Stock Exchange under ticker MVN and the company is headquartered in Carwell, Valorian Republic.

Arcana Cloud Infrastructure, Inc. priced a follow-on public offering of 11.0 million shares of Class A common stock at $78.00 per share on January 9, 2026, for gross proceeds of $858 million. The offering comprised 7.5 million newly issued primary shares and 3.5 million secondary shares sold by early investor Brightfield Technology Ventures LP. Net primary proceeds of approximately $572 million will be deployed to fund capital expenditures associated with Arcana's next-generation sovereign cloud infrastructure initiative, covering construction of 14 classified-capable data centers under long-term government contracts totaling $4.1 billion in aggregate contract value. Strathmore Capital Markets and Garvey Lynch & Co. acted as joint book-running managers. The offering priced at a 4.8 percent discount to the prior closing price of $81.95 on the Aldermoor Exchange. Brightfield Technology Ventures LP's ownership stake was reduced from 14.2 percent to 9.9 percent following the offering. Arcana reported fourth-quarter revenues of $387 million, representing 52 percent year-over-year growth, with an annualized revenue run rate of approximately $1.55 billion as of December 31, 2025. Remaining insiders and employees holding vested options and restricted stock units are subject to a standard 60-day lock-up restriction expiring March 10, 2026.

Relantis Offshore Wind Development Corp. completed a $750 million green bond offering on April 19, 2026, issuing 5.250 percent senior secured notes due 2038 at par pursuant to its established Green Finance Framework, which was independently verified by Nordmark ESG Assurance GmbH. Joint book-running managers Briarwood Securities and Ravensdale Capital led the offering, which attracted a final order book of approximately $3.1 billion from more than 180 institutional investors, including 64 percent allocated to dedicated ESG and responsible investment mandates. The notes are secured by a first-priority pledge of the equity interests of Relantis Offshore Wind Development Corp.'s project subsidiaries, which hold construction and operating permits for 2,150 megawatts of fixed-bottom offshore wind capacity in the Northgate Exclusivity Zone. Proceeds will fund approximately 60 percent of the construction cost of the Noravia Seabreeze Phase II project, with the balance provided by equity contributions from project sponsors and a €280 million subordinated green loan from the Valorian Development Finance Institution. The notes are expected to be rated Baa3 by Thornfield Ratings Services, qualifying as investment grade and eligible for a broad range of institutional investment mandates. Relantis reported consolidated revenues of $484 million from existing operational wind assets for fiscal year 2025.

Halston Precision Medical Devices, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 10.0 million shares at $32.00 per share on February 28, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $320 million. The offering was managed by sole book-runner Fenwick Meridian Group, with Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Albrecht Whitmore Securities serving as co-managers. The overallotment option covering 1.5 million additional shares was exercised in full on March 4, 2026, raising total gross proceeds to $368 million. Halston develops minimally invasive robotic-assisted surgical platforms for laparoscopic cholecystectomy and colorectal resection procedures, with 412 systems installed across 118 hospital networks in 22 countries as of December 31, 2025. The company reported revenues of $213 million for fiscal year 2025, including $94 million in recurring consumable and service contract revenue, with gross margins of 68 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $338 million will be allocated to regulatory clearance submissions in eight additional national markets, investment in next-generation robotic platform development, and a global sales force expansion adding approximately 140 positions. Shares opened at $44.00 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker HPMD, representing a 37.5 percent first-day premium, and closed at $41.20. All insider shares are subject to a 180-day lock-up expiring August 27, 2026.

Crownthorpe Industrial Holdings LLC closed a $1.35 billion leveraged loan facility on March 7, 2026, consisting of a $1.05 billion seven-year term loan B and a $300 million five-year revolving credit facility, to finance the leveraged buyout of Crownthorpe by private equity sponsor Veltermonde Capital Partners Fund VII. Administrative agent Harcastle Investment Bank coordinated the syndication, with Strathmore Capital Markets and Dunmore Equity Partners as joint lead arrangers. The term loan was priced at SORF plus 375 basis points with a 0.75 percent floor and issued at 98.50 cents on the dollar. Total commitments from 74 institutional lenders exceeded $2.1 billion, reflecting approximately 1.56 times oversubscription of the aggregate credit facility. Crownthorpe manufactures specialty engineered fasteners, precision stampings, and structural components for automotive OEM and aerospace Tier-1 customers, generating revenues of $1.9 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $310 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. Pro forma total net leverage at transaction close is estimated at 4.35 times. The revolving credit facility bears interest at SORF plus 300 basis points. Veltermonde Capital Partners Fund VII contributed total equity of approximately $740 million, representing 35.4 percent of total capitalization.

Novalith Payment Technologies, Inc. completed its initial public offering on April 11, 2026, pricing 22.0 million shares of common stock at $19.00 per share for gross proceeds of $418 million on the Aldermoor Exchange under ticker NVLT. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Briarwood Securities managed the offering. The IPO was 10.8 times oversubscribed on a net demand basis after accounting for investor attrition from the higher end of the indicative $16.00 to $19.00 range. Novalith operates a real-time payment processing network connecting 3,100 financial institutions in 44 countries, settling approximately $890 billion in annualized payment volume as of the fourth quarter of 2025. The company reported revenues of $411 million for fiscal year 2025, with a net income margin of 18.4 percent, marking its fourth consecutive year of profitability. Net proceeds of approximately $384 million will fund international network expansion into 18 additional sovereign markets, capital investment in ISO 22022 compliance infrastructure, and potential strategic acquisitions of smaller domestic payment networks. Shares closed at $24.80 on the first trading day, an increase of 30.5 percent above the offering price. The overallotment option covering 3.3 million shares at $19.00 per share is exercisable for 30 days following the IPO date. Lock-up restrictions apply for 180 days to all insiders.

Terravance Agricultural Credit Corp. priced $350 million aggregate principal amount of 5.625 percent farm mortgage bonds due 2031 on January 29, 2026, in a fully registered public offering under the Valorian Securities Disclosure Act. Sole managing underwriter Ravensdale Capital led the transaction, which attracted final orders of approximately $810 million from a diversified base of insurance companies, pension funds, and agricultural lenders. The bonds were priced at 99.875 percent of par to yield 5.646 percent, representing a spread of 187 basis points over the comparable benchmark government bond. Terravance provides mortgage financing to owner-operators of row-crop farmland, fruit orchards, and protected cultivation facilities across the agricultural heartland provinces of Noravia. The company's managed loan portfolio totaled $6.2 billion as of December 31, 2025, with a 90-day delinquency rate of 0.48 percent, reflecting conservative underwriting standards and strong collateral coverage averaging 62 percent loan-to-value. Net proceeds of approximately $346 million will replace maturing commercial paper obligations and fund new mortgage loan originations. The bonds are secured by a pool of first-lien agricultural mortgage loans and carry a rating of AA- from Meridian Credit Ratings and Aa3 from Thornfield Ratings Services.

Peraxis Digital Therapeutics, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 8.5 million shares at $21.00 per share on March 6, 2026, for gross proceeds of $178.5 million. Strathmore Capital Markets served as sole book-running manager, with Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group as co-managers. Peraxis develops prescription digital therapeutics for the adjunctive treatment of major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety disorder, using app-based cognitive behavioral therapy protocols supported by artificial intelligence clinical decision engines. The company reported product-related revenues of $39 million for fiscal year 2025 and ended the year with cash and equivalents of $88 million. Net proceeds of approximately $163 million, together with existing cash, are expected to fund operating activities through the fourth quarter of 2028, including the commercial launch of PDX-201 in the Aldermoor and Valorian markets following receipt of regulatory clearance. Shares opened at $28.50 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker PRDX, closing at $26.10 on the first day, a 24.3 percent first-day premium. The overallotment option covering 1.275 million shares was exercised in full on March 10, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $205.3 million. All officers, directors, and pre-IPO stockholders are subject to a 180-day lock-up expiring September 2, 2026.

Colverdale Metals & Mining Ltd. completed a C$580 million bought-deal public offering of common shares on February 18, 2026, selling 22.3 million shares at C$26.00 per share through a syndicate led by Strathmore Capital Markets (Noravia) and Albrecht Whitmore Securities, with Briarwood Securities and Ravensdale Capital as co-underwriters. The offering was structured as an overnight bought deal, with underwriters purchasing the shares on a firm-commitment basis at C$25.35 per share and reselling at C$26.00. A C$87 million overallotment option covering 3.35 million shares at the offering price was exercised in full on February 21, 2026, bringing total proceeds to C$667 million. Colverdale is a Noravia-based copper and gold producer with operating mines in three jurisdictions, producing approximately 240,000 tonnes of copper equivalent annually. The company intends to use approximately C$510 million of net proceeds to fund 70 percent of the projected capital cost of the Aldervein Copper Expansion Project, which is expected to increase annual copper production by 85,000 tonnes upon completion in 2029. The remaining proceeds will strengthen the balance sheet by repaying C$157 million of outstanding revolving credit borrowings. Colverdale shares trade on the Valorian Mining Exchange and the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker CVM.

Glenshade Asset Management, Inc. raised $230 million in an upsized initial public offering on May 8, 2026, selling 12.8 million shares at $18.00 per share, above the $15.00 to $17.00 preliminary range. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Dunmore Equity Partners. The overallotment option covering 1.92 million additional shares was exercised in full on May 13, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $264.6 million. Glenshade is a multi-strategy alternative asset manager with $42.3 billion in assets under management across hedge funds, private credit vehicles, and real assets strategies. Management fee revenues totaled $387 million for fiscal year 2025, with performance and incentive fees of $211 million. Net proceeds of approximately $242 million will be used to seed three new fund strategies, fund a technology platform upgrade across the firm's portfolio management and risk systems, and finance minority stake acquisitions in two complementary boutique investment managers. Shares began trading on the Aldermoor Exchange under the ticker GNSH, opening at $24.50 and closing at $22.80 on the first day, a 26.7 percent first-day gain. The company's founding partners, holding 61.4 percent of pre-offering shares in aggregate, are subject to a 365-day lock-up restriction.

Solvaren Energy Finance LLC priced a $600 million term loan B facility on April 25, 2026, bearing interest at SORF plus 300 basis points with no floor, issued at an original issue discount of 99.50 cents on the dollar, implying an all-in yield at issuance of approximately 8.10 percent. Harcastle Investment Bank served as administrative agent and sole structuring bank, with Briarwood Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group as joint lead arrangers. The facility was approximately 1.9 times oversubscribed at the time of allocation, reflecting strong investor demand from CLO managers and bank loan mutual funds. Solvaren intends to use proceeds to refinance in full its existing $575 million term loan B due 2027, which bore interest at SORF plus 450 basis points, and to pay transaction-related fees and expenses. The transaction extends Solvaren's debt maturity profile from 1.9 years to 6.2 years and reduces annual cash interest expense by approximately $8.7 million based on current SORF rates. Solvaren operates a 2,200-mile integrated natural gas liquids pipeline and fractionation network in the Granville Basin, generating EBITDA of $188 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. The facility is rated BB from Thornfield Ratings Services and carries standard financial covenant protections including a 5.5 times total leverage covenant.

Falconcrest Defense & Technology Corp. priced its initial public offering of 17.0 million shares of common stock at $25.00 per share on March 18, 2026, for gross proceeds of $425 million. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Strathmore Capital Markets and Albrecht Whitmore Securities. Falconcrest develops hardened electronic warfare suites, advanced radar systems, and autonomous surveillance drone platforms for military and homeland-security customers in NATO-aligned sovereign nations. The company reported revenues of $847 million for fiscal year 2025, with a backlog of $3.4 billion as of December 31, 2025. Net proceeds of approximately $390 million will fund capacity expansion at the company's Montclair Avionics manufacturing complex, accelerate delivery timelines on existing government contracts, and invest in third-generation phased-array radar development. Shares were admitted to trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker FCDT and closed at $31.50 on the first day of trading, a 26.0 percent premium to the offering price. The overallotment option covering 2.55 million shares was exercised in full on March 21, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $488.75 million. All pre-IPO shareholders, including employee stock plan participants, are subject to a 180-day lock-up expiring September 14, 2026. The offering was 8.4 times oversubscribed.

Westland Fiber Networks, Inc. completed the sale of $900 million aggregate principal amount of 5.750 percent senior secured first-lien notes due 2034 on February 10, 2026, in a private offering conducted under Rule 144A and Regulation S. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Ravensdale Capital priced the notes at 99.500 percent of par. Westland is a Noravia-based fiber-to-the-premises broadband provider serving approximately 1.9 million residential and 87,000 business subscribers across 41 metropolitan markets, with a total network footprint encompassing 28,400 route miles of fiber. The company reported revenues of $785 million and adjusted EBITDA of $348 million for fiscal year 2025, implying a pro forma net leverage ratio of approximately 4.0 times following the offering. Proceeds of approximately $890 million will be used to fund the Westland Accelerate 2028 network densification and footprint expansion program, targeting 650,000 incremental premises passed at an estimated capital intensity of $980 per home. The notes are secured on a first-lien basis by substantially all assets of Westland Fiber Networks and its material subsidiaries. Thornfield Ratings Services assigned a provisional BB rating to the notes, while Meridian Credit Ratings assigned BB-. Westland is 62 percent owned by private equity firm Castlegate Infrastructure Partners Fund III.

Brighthollow Consumer Goods Corp. priced a secondary offering of 7.5 million shares of common stock at $44.00 per share on April 28, 2026, for gross proceeds of $330 million. The offering was structured entirely as a secondary sale by selling shareholder Drayfield Capital Partners Fund V, with the company receiving no proceeds. Sole book-runner Fenwick Meridian Group priced the offering at a 5.2 percent discount to Brighthollow's prior-day close of $46.41 on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker BHCG. Drayfield Capital Partners Fund V's ownership position was reduced from 27.3 percent to 16.8 percent of outstanding shares following the offering. Brighthollow manufactures and markets premium pet nutrition products, home fragrance goods, and artisanal snack foods under 11 proprietary brands distributed through 62,000 retail locations and its own direct-to-consumer e-commerce platforms. The company reported net revenues of $1.18 billion for fiscal year 2025, with adjusted EBITDA of $212 million, representing an 18.0 percent margin. Brighthollow also announced separately that its board of directors authorized a share repurchase program of up to $150 million over the 12 months following the secondary offering, which management indicated will be funded from free cash flow. Remaining insiders are subject to a 60-day post-offering lock-up.

Paragon Cellular Holdings, Inc. completed the pricing of a $2.5 billion dual-tranche senior unsecured notes offering on March 26, 2026, consisting of $1.25 billion aggregate principal amount of 5.100 percent notes due 2031 and $1.25 billion aggregate principal amount of 5.600 percent notes due 2036. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Harcastle Investment Bank, Strathmore Capital Markets, Dunmore Equity Partners, and Pelletier & Wynne Securities. The 2031 notes were priced at 99.875 percent of par to yield 5.125 percent, and the 2036 notes were priced at 99.625 percent of par to yield 5.637 percent, representing spreads of 127 and 165 basis points, respectively, over comparable benchmark treasury securities. Total orders exceeded $7.8 billion across both tranches. Paragon operates a wireless telecommunications network serving approximately 48 million subscribers in 14 sovereign jurisdictions under the Velantian Economic Cooperative framework. Proceeds of approximately $2.47 billion will be used to refinance $1.9 billion of notes maturing in 2027 and 2028, fund spectrum license acquisitions announced in February 2026, and for general corporate purposes. Paragon Cellular Holdings carries an investment-grade rating of Baa2/BBB from Thornfield Ratings Services and Meridian Credit Ratings, respectively.

Verindal Real Estate Finance, Inc. priced its initial public offering of mortgage investment trust shares on January 14, 2026, selling 24.0 million shares at $20.00 per share for gross proceeds of $480 million. Joint book-running managers Albrecht Whitmore Securities and Briarwood Securities led the offering. The company intends to invest its IPO proceeds, together with leverage from committed credit facilities totaling $850 million, in a diversified portfolio of first-lien commercial real estate bridge loans, mezzanine debt instruments, and preferred equity interests collateralized by transitional income-producing properties. The target portfolio yield at inception is estimated at 9.8 percent on a blended basis, with a projected dividend yield of approximately 8.5 percent based on the offering price, payable quarterly. The overallotment option covering 3.6 million shares at $20.00 was exercised in full on January 18, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $552 million. Shares began trading on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under the ticker VRDF, opening at $20.85 and closing at $20.50 on the first day of trading. The company's external manager, Verindal Capital Management LLC, will receive an annualized base management fee of 1.50 percent of equity and a 20 percent incentive fee on returns above an 8.0 percent hurdle. Officers and directors are subject to a 180-day lock-up restriction.

Stonegate Payments Infrastructure Corp. priced $450 million aggregate principal amount of 4.875 percent senior notes due 2030 on May 1, 2026, in a public offering registered under the Centralia Securities Disclosure Ordinance. The notes were issued at 99.750 percent of par through joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Fenwick Meridian Group, with Ravensdale Capital as co-manager. The offering attracted final demand of approximately $1.6 billion from a geographically diversified investor base. Stonegate operates payment settlement rails, card-switch infrastructure, and ATM network technology serving 1,470 member financial institutions across the Centralia monetary union. The company reported revenues of $562 million for fiscal year 2025, with adjusted EBITDA of $247 million and free cash flow conversion of 88 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $440 million will refinance $395 million of outstanding floating-rate term borrowings and fund $45 million of capital expenditures associated with next-generation ISO 20022-compliant settlement architecture. The notes were assigned an investment-grade rating of Baa3 by Thornfield Ratings Services and BBB- by Meridian Credit Ratings. Stonegate is majority-owned by a consortium of member financial institutions and a 28 percent strategic stake held by Castlegate Infrastructure Partners. Annual interest savings from the transaction are estimated at approximately $6.2 million compared to the replaced floating-rate debt.

Arvolex Biosystems Corp. priced an upsized follow-on offering of 5.5 million shares at $112.00 per share on February 23, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $616 million, including full exercise of the 825,000 share overallotment option. The offering comprised 4.0 million primary shares and 1.5 million secondary shares sold by pre-IPO institutional holders Cascadia Life Sciences Fund II and Goldstream Bioequity Partners. Net primary proceeds of approximately $434 million will fund Phase III clinical trials of ARV-440, a gene-editing therapeutic targeting rare monogenic liver disorders, and to expand the company's proprietary protein-engineering research platform. Strathmore Capital Markets acted as sole book-running manager. The offering priced at a 4.1 percent premium to the prior closing price of $107.60 on the Aldermoor Exchange, reflecting significant inbound investor demand ahead of ARV-440 Phase II data readout scheduled for June 2026. Arvolex shares closed at $121.40 on the day of the offering announcement, up 12.8 percent. The company reported cash and equivalents of $287 million before the offering and estimated its cash runway extended to the first quarter of 2029 following completion of the transaction. Remaining insiders are restricted by a 60-day lock-up agreement. Cascadia Life Sciences Fund II reduced its ownership from 9.3 percent to 5.9 percent.

Redstone Mobility Services, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 20.0 million shares at $14.00 per share on April 16, 2026, for gross proceeds of $280 million on the Centralia Commodities and Securities Exchange under ticker RMSV. Joint book-running managers Pelletier & Wynne Securities and Albrecht Whitmore Securities led the offering. The deal was 7.1 times oversubscribed and priced at the midpoint of the $13.00 to $15.00 preliminary range. Redstone operates an on-demand ride-hailing and micro-mobility sharing platform serving 38 cities in 12 countries, with 8.4 million monthly active riders and 1.6 million registered drivers and mobility operators as of December 31, 2025. The company reported net revenues of $498 million for fiscal year 2025, representing 31 percent growth, and generated adjusted EBITDA of $42 million, its first fiscal year of positive adjusted EBITDA. Net proceeds of approximately $257 million will fund market expansion into 14 additional cities, investment in electric vehicle fleet electrification incentive programs, and technology development including route-optimization AI models. The overallotment option covering 3.0 million additional shares at $14.00 was exercised in full on April 19, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $322 million. Insiders are subject to 180-day lock-ups expiring October 13, 2026.

Lumivax Therapeutics, Inc. completed a $192 million overnight follow-on offering of 8.0 million shares of common stock at $24.00 per share on March 4, 2026, representing a 7.3 percent discount to the prior closing price of $25.89 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange. Sole book-runner Dunmore Equity Partners ran an accelerated overnight book-build that closed within six hours of launch. The offering size was increased from an originally contemplated $150 million due to demand from dedicated healthcare investors and multi-sector long-only funds. Lumivax is developing a pipeline of mRNA-based vaccines targeting neglected tropical diseases and emerging infectious pathogens, with three candidates in Phase II and one in Phase I development. The company reported research collaboration revenues of $38 million for fiscal year 2025 and ended the year with cash and equivalents of $143 million. Net proceeds of approximately $183 million will extend the company's cash runway through mid-2029, fund the initiation of two additional Phase II studies, and support regulatory submission activities for LVX-201, its lead malaria vaccine candidate, in six endemic-country regulatory authorities. The overallotment option covering 1.2 million additional shares was exercised on March 7, 2026. Officers and directors are subject to 90-day lock-up restrictions.

Silvermoor Renewable Power Finance Ltd. priced €600 million aggregate principal amount of 4.250 percent green notes due 2035 on January 20, 2026, in a Regulation S-only offering targeted at European institutional investors. Joint book-running managers Harcastle Investment Bank and Strathmore Capital Markets led the transaction, which attracted orders exceeding €2.9 billion from more than 220 accounts, including sovereign wealth funds, insurance general accounts, and dedicated green bond mandates. The notes were priced at 99.700 percent of par, representing a spread of 165 basis points over the comparable Noravia government benchmark. Silvermoor is a holding company for a portfolio of 34 operational solar and onshore wind projects totaling 3,850 megawatts of installed capacity across six Valorian Economic Zone members. The company reported revenues of €482 million and adjusted EBITDA of €378 million for fiscal year 2025, with a weighted average power purchase agreement tenor of 11.3 remaining years. Proceeds of approximately €594 million will be applied to refinance €450 million of senior secured notes due 2027, fund €100 million of discretionary capital expenditures, and pay transaction costs. The notes received a BBB- rating from Meridian Credit Ratings and a Baa3 rating from Thornfield Ratings Services, placing Silvermoor firmly within the investment-grade universe for the first time following a two-notch upgrade in October 2025.

Kestral Consumer Technology, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 28.0 million shares of common stock at $13.00 per share on February 6, 2026, for gross proceeds of $364 million. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Fenwick Meridian Group. An overallotment option covering 4.2 million shares was partially exercised, with underwriters purchasing 3.5 million shares for additional gross proceeds of $45.5 million, bringing total gross proceeds to $409.5 million. Kestral designs and sells smart home security systems, AI-enabled door locks, and connected environmental sensors through retail channels and a direct subscription security monitoring service. The company reported revenues of $519 million for fiscal year 2025, with 71 percent recurring revenue from monitoring and software subscriptions and a net dollar retention rate of 108 percent. Kestral reported adjusted EBITDA of $78 million for fiscal year 2025, representing a 15.0 percent margin. Net proceeds of approximately $375 million will fund retail expansion into mass-market channels, subsidize hardware bundling with multi-year monitoring contracts, and invest in AI-native threat detection software capabilities. Shares closed at $15.70 on the first trading day on the Aldermoor Exchange under ticker KSRL, a 20.8 percent first-day gain. All insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions expiring August 5, 2026.

Torrel Specialty Finance Corp. completed the offering of $325 million aggregate principal amount of 6.750 percent senior unsecured notes due 2029 on March 13, 2026, in a private placement to qualified institutional buyers pursuant to Rule 144A. Sole book-running manager Briarwood Securities priced the notes at par. Torrel is a specialty finance company focused on originating and managing senior secured loans to middle-market companies in the Centralia and Valorian economic zones, with a managed loan portfolio of $4.1 billion across 196 borrowers as of December 31, 2025. The company reported net investment income of $0.86 per share for fiscal year 2025 and paid dividends totaling $0.84 per share, representing a distribution rate of 7.2 percent on average net asset value. Net proceeds of approximately $318 million will be used to repay $250 million of revolving credit borrowings drawn to fund new loan originations, with the remaining approximately $68 million retained to fund additional originations and general corporate purposes. The notes are unsecured obligations of Torrel Specialty Finance Corp. and are not guaranteed by any portfolio company. The notes received a rating of BBB- from Meridian Credit Ratings, consistent with Torrel's investment-grade profile maintained since fiscal year 2021. Annual interest expense is expected to be approximately $21.9 million.

Meridian Ports Authority Trust completed a $700 million infrastructure bond offering on April 5, 2026, issuing 4.625 percent revenue bonds due 2046 in a public transaction registered under the Valorian Municipal Securities Transparency Ordinance. Albrecht Whitmore Securities and Ravensdale Capital served as joint senior managers, with Pelletier & Wynne Securities as co-senior manager. The bonds were priced at 99.250 percent of par to yield 4.665 percent, representing a spread of 142 basis points over the 20-year benchmark Valorian sovereign bond yield of 3.245 percent. The offering attracted final orders from 104 institutional investors including pension funds, insurance companies, and infrastructure-focused closed-end funds. Meridian Ports Authority Trust is the statutory financing authority for the Meridian Metropolitan Port Complex, the largest containerized cargo facility in the Central Valorian coastal region, handling 6.4 million twenty-foot equivalent units annually and generating operating revenues of $498 million for fiscal year 2025. Bond proceeds of approximately $692 million will fund Phase III of the port's capacity expansion and modernization program, encompassing construction of two additional deep-water berths capable of accommodating ultra-large container vessels and installation of 18 electrified automated stacking cranes. The bonds are secured by a pledge of port operating revenues and carry an AA rating from Meridian Credit Ratings.

Halcyon Materials Science, Inc. priced a $250 million aggregate principal amount of 1.750 percent convertible senior notes due 2030 on January 31, 2026, in a Rule 144A private placement. Joint book-running managers Strathmore Capital Markets and Dunmore Equity Partners led the offering. The notes were priced at par with an initial conversion premium of 35 percent above the reference price of $67.40 per Halcyon share, establishing an initial conversion price of approximately $91.00. The initial purchasers were also granted an option to purchase up to $37.5 million in additional principal amount of notes, which was exercised in full, bringing total proceeds to $287.5 million. Halcyon intends to use net proceeds of approximately $281 million to fund the development and scale-up of its proprietary solid-state electrolyte manufacturing process, including construction of a 200,000 square foot pilot-to-production facility at the Greenbrooke Technology Park. The company simultaneously entered into capped call transactions with a cap price of approximately $134.80 per share, representing a 100 percent premium to the reference share price. Halcyon reported revenues of $94 million for fiscal year 2025, primarily from government-sponsored research contracts, with total liquidity of $178 million prior to the offering. Shares of Halcyon trade on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker HLCY.

Granwood Residential Communities, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 15.0 million shares of common stock at $22.00 per share on April 24, 2026, for gross proceeds of $330 million. Joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co. and Harcastle Investment Bank led the transaction. Granwood owns and operates a portfolio of 47 single-family rental communities comprising 6,840 homes across 11 Sunbelt and Mountain West submarkets in the Valorian Federal territories, targeting workforce-demographic residents with median household incomes between $65,000 and $110,000 annually. The company reported total revenues of $387 million, same-community net operating income growth of 7.2 percent, and adjusted funds from operations of $1.38 per diluted share for fiscal year 2025, with a year-end portfolio occupancy rate of 96.4 percent. Net proceeds of approximately $303 million will fund the acquisition and development of approximately 1,200 additional homes currently under contract or in pre-development. The overallotment option covering 2.25 million shares was exercised in full on April 28, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $379.5 million. Shares began trading on the Aldermoor Exchange under ticker GRWC, opening at $25.50 and closing at $24.10, a 9.5 percent first-day gain. All insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions expiring October 21, 2026.

Portsmith Logistics Corp. raised $1.0 billion through a high-yield bond offering on March 28, 2026, issuing $600 million aggregate principal amount of 8.000 percent first-lien senior secured notes due 2031 and $400 million aggregate principal amount of 10.250 percent second-lien senior secured notes due 2032. Joint book-running managers Briarwood Securities and Fenwick Meridian Group priced the first-lien notes at 99.00 percent and the second-lien notes at 98.00 percent of par. Portsmith is a provider of contract logistics, warehousing, and last-mile delivery services with 108 distribution centers aggregating 38 million square feet of warehouse space across the Valorian and Noravia freight networks. The company was acquired by private equity sponsor Talbourne Capital Partners in a leveraged transaction in 2023 for a total enterprise value of $2.8 billion. Portsmith reported revenues of $3.1 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $275 million for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. Pro forma total debt following the offering is approximately $1.09 billion, implying total net leverage of 3.78 times EBITDA. Proceeds will be used to refinance existing credit facilities, pay transaction fees and expenses of approximately $42 million, and distribute $148 million to equity holders. The first-lien notes were rated B+ by Thornfield Ratings Services; the second-lien notes were rated B-.

Orenthal Advanced Diagnostics, Inc. priced its initial public offering of 9.0 million shares at $29.00 per share on February 25, 2026, generating gross proceeds of $261 million. Sole book-runner Pelletier & Wynne Securities priced the offering at the top of the $26.00 to $29.00 preliminary range following an oversubscribed roadshow. The overallotment option covering 1.35 million shares was exercised in full on March 1, 2026, bringing total gross proceeds to $300.15 million. Orenthal develops point-of-care molecular diagnostic platforms using proprietary microfluidic cartridge technology capable of delivering multiplex pathogen identification results within 22 minutes in decentralized care settings. The company's installed base comprised 8,700 instruments across 54 countries as of December 31, 2025, with consumable revenue of $178 million and instrument revenue of $64 million in fiscal year 2025. Net proceeds of approximately $275 million will fund commercialization of two additional instrument platforms currently in pre-commercial production, expand the international field service organization by 230 positions, and fund performance of a post-market clinical registry study required by regulators in four jurisdictions. Shares opened at $40.00 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker ORND and closed at $37.50, a 29.3 percent first-day gain. All insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions.

Tavenridge Grid Technologies, Inc. completed an underwritten offering of 6.5 million shares of common stock at $55.00 per share on May 7, 2026, for gross proceeds of $357.5 million. The offering comprised 5.0 million newly issued shares from the company and 1.5 million secondary shares sold by pre-IPO shareholder Irongate Ventures Fund III. Sole book-runner Strathmore Capital Markets led the offering, which priced at a 3.9 percent discount to the prior-day closing price of $57.24 on the Aldermoor Exchange. Net primary proceeds of approximately $267 million will fund an accelerated production ramp-up of Tavenridge's grid-scale solid-state battery storage systems and the establishment of a second manufacturing facility at the Aldervein Industrial Campus, with an expected annual production capacity of 4.0 gigawatt-hours upon full commissioning in the fourth quarter of 2027. Tavenridge reported revenues of $318 million for fiscal year 2025, including $211 million from utility-scale storage system deliveries and $107 million from grid-integration software licensing. The company reported a backlog of $1.64 billion as of March 31, 2026. Irongate Ventures Fund III's ownership stake declined from 11.8 percent to 8.6 percent following the offering. Remaining officers and directors are subject to a 90-day lock-up restriction expiring August 5, 2026.

Delvecchio Aerospace Systems Corp. priced its initial public offering of 19.5 million shares of common stock at $21.00 per share on March 20, 2026, for gross proceeds of $409.5 million. The offering was led by joint book-running managers Garvey Lynch & Co., Harcastle Investment Bank, and Albrecht Whitmore Securities. The transaction was 12.3 times oversubscribed, with final orders received from 310 institutional accounts including a significant allocation to sovereign wealth funds. The IPO priced above the $18.00 to $20.00 per share preliminary range established during the international roadshow. Delvecchio manufactures satellite bus platforms, payload integration systems, and launch vehicle adapters for commercial, government, and scientific satellite customers, with a contract backlog of $2.9 billion as of December 31, 2025. The company reported revenues of $763 million for fiscal year 2025, growing 28 percent year-over-year, and adjusted EBITDA of $148 million, a 19.4 percent margin. Net proceeds of approximately $376 million will fund the construction of a new low-earth-orbit satellite manufacturing line with annual throughput capacity of 120 bus platforms, invest in proprietary electric propulsion development, and retire $85 million of mezzanine debt. Shares opened at $30.50 on the Transcontinental Securities Exchange under ticker DVCA, closing at $28.90 on the first trading day, a 37.6 percent gain. Insiders are subject to 180-day lock-up restrictions expiring September 16, 2026.
